risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Randomization to the WFI or UC group occurred next using an automated 1:1 scheme for the overall sample" Comment: This describes an automated allocation scheme with randomization, which is sufficient to suggest a proper random sequence was used. The additional context clues "RCT" and "randomizing" support a genuine randomized allocation process rather than a predictable rule.

_evidence.method_quote: Randomization to the WFI or UC group occurred next using an automated 1:1 scheme for the overall sample; sample sizes were comparable (85 vs. 82) for the subgroup.
_evidence.method_summary: Participants were randomized 1:1 to WFI or usual care using an automated scheme.

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Randomization to the WFI or UC group occurred next using an automated 1:1 scheme for the overall sample;" Comment: The article explicitly describes an automated allocation scheme, which functions as central allocation and would prevent the recruiter from foreseeing the next assignment. Although it does not mention envelopes or another manual method, the automated scheme is sufficient explicit evidence of concealment.

_evidence.concealment_quote: Randomization to the WFI or UC group occurred next using an automated 1:1 scheme for the overall sample;
_evidence.note: The article describes an automated allocation scheme but does not mention envelopes or any other manual concealment process.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "They were neither blinded to the intervention and the UC group received no direct contact from a study provider." Comment: Participants and personnel were explicitly not blinded. This could plausibly influence the reported outcomes because the study measured multiple subjective, self-reported endpoints, including presenteeism, WLQ task limitations, self-reported absences, and PHQ-9 depression severity, all of which could be affected by knowledge of assignment.

_evidence.blinding_quote: They were neither blinded to the intervention and the UC group received no direct contact from a study provider.

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "To minimize response bias all evaluations were completed on line rather than by in person interview; however it is possible that study participants who received the WFI were positively biased in their reporting. They were neither blinded to the intervention and the UC group received no direct contact from a study provider." Comment: The outcomes were assessed by patient self-report, and participants were not blinded. Because the reported outcomes include multiple subjective patient-reported measures (WLQ productivity/limitations and PHQ-9), knowledge of allocation could directly influence outcome measurement. Under the worst-outcome rule, the unblinded subjective/self-reported outcomes make the domain high risk.

_evidence.assessor_blinding_quote: To minimize response bias all evaluations were completed on line rather than by in person interview; however it is possible that study participants who received the WFI were positively biased in their reporting. They were neither blinded to the intervention and the UC group received no direct contact from a study provider.

outcomes.label: self-reported absences / WLQ Time Loss Module

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Seven (8%) employees in the WFI group and 10 (12%) of the UC group did not complete the self-report follow-up questionnaire and were considered lost to follow-up." Comment: The primary analysed outcome was assessed at 4 months, with per-arm analysed numbers reported (85 vs 78 and 82 vs 72). Total loss was 10.2% with only a 4.5 percentage-point difference between arms, and the reported reasons were generic loss to follow-up rather than outcome-related. The analysis also used mixed effects modeling/ITT with a LOCF sensitivity analysis, which supports a low risk judgement.

per_arm_flow.reasons: “did not complete the self-report follow-up questionnaire and were considered lost to follow-up”
“did not complete the self-report follow-up questionnaire and were considered lost to follow-up”

_evidence.missing_data_method: ITT; “mixed effects modeling”; “A sensitivity analysis was done on drop-out ... last observation carried forward (LOCF)”
_evidence.notes: Attrition numbers are reported for the subgroup analysis and correspond to the primary outcome follow-up at 4 months (WLQ at-work productivity loss).
